rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) studying** ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) studying** ...

content.subtitle: Use
items: Fixed future arrangements: We **'re meeting** our cousins on Saturday.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: We use *be going to* for intentions that are not fully arranged yet. ✗ I **'m becoming** a pilot one day.
We do not normally use the present continuous for predictions. ✗ I think it **'s snowing** tomorrow.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Predictions and opinions: I think people **will live** longer in the future.
Offers and promises: I **'ll help** you with your homework.
Instant decisions: I'm thirsty — I **'ll get** some water.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use *shall* for offers and suggestions with *I* and *we*: **Shall** we go now?
We do not usually use *will* for planned arrangements.

rows.label: statement
rows.right: He/she/it **is ('s) going to study** ... You/we/they **are ('re) going to study** ...

rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) going to study** ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) going to study** ...

rows.label: question
rows.right: **Is** he/she/it **going to study** ...? **Are** you/we/they **going to study** ...?

content.subtitle: Use
items: Future intentions: She **'s going to learn** Japanese next year.
Predictions based on evidence: Look at those clouds — it **'s going to rain**.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Timetables and schedules: The train **arrives** at half past six.

sections.title: A: Present continuous (Emma's diary)
sections.instructions: Look at Emma's diary and use the prompts to write sentences. Use the correct form of the present continuous.

items.sentence: On Monday, she ......
items.answer: is meeting Josh at the school café

items.sentence: On Tuesday, she ......
items.answer: is going swimming with her cousin

items.sentence: On Wednesday, she ......
items.answer: is taking the bus to the city centre

items.sentence: On Thursday, she ......
items.answer: is spending the afternoon with her grandparents

items.sentence: On Friday, she ......
items.answer: is flying to Rome at 8 pm

items.sentence: On Saturday, she ......
items.answer: is helping Mum in the kitchen

sections.title: B: will or shall
sections.instructions: Complete using will or shall and the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: I think it ...... later this evening.
Don't worry — I ...... you with your homework.
...... you ...... me after school?
We ...... to a bigger house next year.
...... we ...... outside the cinema for you?
I think Sam ...... us the photos tomorrow.
I ...... a sandwich, please.
Don't worry. They ...... us at the station.

sections.title: C: be going to
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of be going to and the verbs in brackets. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: I ...... (study) engineering at university.
They ...... (paint) their bedroom this weekend.
...... Ben ...... (apply) for that job?
We ...... (not / stay) at home this evening.
Look out! You ...... (drop) those books!
I ...... (start) a new course next month.
...... they ...... (visit) their cousins in July?
Dad ...... (not / drive) to work tomorrow.

sections.title: D: Present simple
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present simple form of the verbs in the box.
sections.passage: Our coach (1) ...... at half past seven in the morning and we (2) ...... the ferry at ten o'clock. We (3) ...... in France around midday and we (4) ...... home late on Sunday evening.

sections.title: E: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: I think Ella **will become / is becoming** a great doctor one day.
We **are meeting / will meet** our teacher after class today.
What **are you going to wear / do you wear** to the party tonight?
**Will you help / Shall you help** me carry these bags?
My brother **is going to study / will study** medicine at university.
We **are having / will have** a science test tomorrow morning.
I'm sure you **will enjoy / are enjoying** the concert.
**Are they travelling / Do they travel** to Italy next month?
I think our team **will win / is winning** the competition.
If you like, I **will speak / am speaking** to the teacher for you.
'Have you arranged your holiday yet?' 'Yes. ...... to Greece in August.'
'I can't carry all these books.' 'Don't worry. ...... you.'
'Can you pass me the salt?' 'Sure. ...... it to you.'
'What are your plans for next year?' '...... a photography course.'
'Do you think Mia will win?' 'Yes, I think ...... the race easily.'
'Look at those dark clouds!' 'Yes, I think ...... soon.'
